title: Blue
content:
Standin' at de winder,
    Feelin' kind o' glum,
    Listenin' to de raindrops
    Play de kettle drum,
    Lookin' crost de medders
    Swimmin' lak a sea;
    Lawd 'a' mussy on us,
    What's de good o' me?

    Can't go out a-hoein',
    Wouldn't ef I could;
    Groun' too wet fu' huntin',
    Fishin' ain't no good.
    Too much noise fo' sleepin',
    No one hyeah to chat;
    Des mus' stan' an' listen
    To dat pit-a-pat.

    Hills is gittin' misty,,
    Valley's gittin' dahk;
    Watch-dog's 'mence a-howlin',
    Rathah have 'em ba'k
    Dan a-moanin' solemn
    Somewhaih out o' sight;
    Rain-crow des a-chucklin'--
    Dis is his delight.

    Mandy, bring my banjo,
    Bring de chillen in,
    Come in f'om de kitchen,
    I feel sick ez sin.
    Call in Uncle Isaac,
    Call Aunt Hannah, too,
    Tain't no use in talkin',
    Chile, I's sholy blue.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Paul Laurence Dunbar delivers a powerful performance in "Blue"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
